llcFilesWith: Maine Secretary of State, Division of Corporations (Statement of Intention to Transact Business Under an Assumed or Fictitious Name, Form ASUM)
soleProprietorFilesWith: Municipal clerk of the city or town where the business is located (Maine has no state-level trade name filing for sole proprietors or general partnerships; they deposit a sworn certificate with the municipal clerk before commencing business, 31 M.R.S. §§ 1-2)
feeBasis: The Secretary of State fee for an LLC's assumed name filing is $125. A sole proprietor's certificate is filed with the municipal clerk, whose fee varies by town.
renewalRule: An LLC's assumed name stays on file until the company files to change or cancel it; there is no periodic renewal.
protectionNote: An assumed name or municipal trade name discloses the name a business uses and grants no exclusive right to it.
searchNote: LLC assumed names filed with the Secretary of State appear in the corporate search. Sole-proprietor trade names sit only in municipal clerk records, so a full check means contacting the relevant town or city.
notes: Maine splits its DBA system. An LLC files a Statement of Intention to Transact Business Under an Assumed or Fictitious Name with the Secretary of State ($125). Sole proprietors and general partnerships have no state-level option: they must deposit a sworn certificate with the municipal clerk of the city or town where they do business before commencing (31 M.R.S. §§ 1-2), so a full search of individual DBAs means checking the relevant municipality. Neither filing grants an exclusive right, and neither requires publication.
